Recent papers

Recent papers on the n2+1 prime conjecture, surfaced by the prominence of their authors in this directory.

Showing 15 papers, ranked by the Top 100 standing of their authors then by recency. Authors who appear in the Top 100 are shown in bold.

#TitleAuthorsPublishedBest rank
1Skolem Meets Bateman-HornFlorian Luca, James Maynard, Armand Noubissie, Joël Ouaknine, James Worrell2023-08#1
2Primitive root bias for twin primes II: Schinzel-type theorems for totient quotients and the sum-of-divisors functionStephan Ramon Garcia, Florian Luca, Kye Shi, Gabe Udell2019-06#1
3Primitive root biases for prime pairs I: existence and non-totality of biasesStephan Ramon Garcia, Florian Luca, Timothy Schaaff2017-08#1
4Primitive root bias for twin primesStephan Ramon Garcia, Elvis Kahoro, Florian Luca2017-05#1
5Bateman-Horn, polynomial Chowla and the Hasse principle with probability 1Tim Browning, Efthymios Sofos, Joni Teräväinen2022-12#8
6Schinzel Hypothesis on average and rational pointsAlexei N. Skorobogatov, Efthymios Sofos2020-05#8
7Cyclotomic polynomials with prescribed height and prime number theoryAlexandre Kosyak, Pieter Moree, Efthymios Sofos, Bin Zhang2019-10#8
8Rational points and prime values of polynomials in moderately many variablesKevin Destagnol, Efthymios Sofos2018-01#8
9Serre's problem on the density of isotropic fibres in conic bundlesEfthymios Sofos2016-02#8
10Level of distribution of unbalanced convolutionsÉtienne Fouvry, Maksym Radziwiłł2018-11#10
11On the exponent of distribution of the ternary divisor functionÉtienne Fouvry, Emmanuel Kowalski, Philippe Michel2013-04#10
12Fibonacci primes, primes of the form $2^n-k$ and beyondJon Grantham, Andrew Granville2023-07#15
13An average Brun-Titchmarsh theorem and shifted primes with a large prime factorRunbo Li2025-08#16
14Largest square divisors of shifted primesRunbo Li2025-05#16
15On the largest prime factor of quadratic polynomialsRunbo Li2024-06#16

Most cited (all time)

The ten most-cited Mathematics works on the n2+1 prime conjecture, by citation count in OpenAlex. Results are filtered to the Mathematics field (OpenAlex field 26) and ranked by total citations.

The Top 100 ranking is designed to capture prominent, currently active researchers. If an author in this table is not linked to a profile, it is usually because they are deceased or less active than the researchers in the ranking, not because they are less significant to the field.

#TitleAuthorsYearCitations
1Almost-primes represented by quadratic polynomials DOIHenryk Iwaniec1978136
2Primes represented by quadratic polynomials in two variables DOIHenryk Iwaniec197454
3Mean values of Dirichlet polynomials and applications to linear equations with prime variables DOIStephen Choi; Angel Kumchev200637
4A Report on Primes of the Form k⋅2 n + 1 and On Factors of Fermat Numbers DOIRaphael M. Robinson195832
5Primes represented by irreducible polynomials in one variable DOIPaul T. Bateman; Roger A. Horn196531
6New quadratic polynomials with high densities of prime values DOIMichael J. Jacobson; Hugh C. Williams200229
7Primes are nonnegative values of a polynomial in 10 variables DOIYu. V. Matijasevič198127
8On prime valued polynomials and class numbers of real quadratic fields DOIR. A. Mollin; Hywel C Williams198827
9Polynomial values free of large prime factors DOICécile Dartyge; Gérald Tenenbaum; Greg Martin200224
10Quadratic polynomials which have a high density of prime values DOIGilbert Fung; Hywel C Williams199024

Maintained by Steve Hubbard (admin@wwin2p1.org). Data sources: arXiv, OpenAlex, zbMATH Open, Mathematics Genealogy Project.

Last built June 2026.
